data.description:
## Context
rebar config is currently a BESPOKE flat key=value file (.rebar/config.conf / .rebar.conf / $REBAR_CONFIG) read by ad-hoc inline parsers in several modules, plus ~40 scattered os.environ.get reads with no single source of truth. A subagent researched what popular, actively-maintained Python CLI/dev-tools (ruff, black, mypy, pytest, pip, uv, poetry, coverage) have converged on. This epic aligns rebar with those conventions.

## Research-backed target (community convergence)
- FORMAT: TOML, namespaced under [tool.rebar] in pyproject.toml + optional standalone rebar.toml, read via stdlib tomllib. (PEP 518/621/680.) Replaces the bespoke flat parser (which silently mishandles "=" in values, has no types/nesting, and drops unknown keys silently).
- DISCOVERY: walk UP from cwd to nearest project config (stop at rebar.toml / [tool.rebar] / .git / fs root); fall back to user-level XDG (~/.config/rebar/) only when no project config exists.
- PRECEDENCE (documented): CLI flag > REBAR_<KEY> env var > project config > user config > built-in defaults.
- ENV: standardize on REBAR_<KEY> prefix; REBAR_CONFIG already points at a config-file path (keep). Secrets (JIRA_API_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) stay in env / gitignored .env, NOT the committed config.
- SAFETY: single typed Config object as source of truth; reject/warn on unknown keys (typo guard, vs todays silent drop); fail-closed on parse error (already done for the verify gate); alias old keys during a deprecation window; a `rebar config`/--show-config command for precedence transparency.

## HARD CONSTRAINT (rebar-specific)
Core rebar is stdlib-only (pyproject dependencies = []). So the research s pydantic-settings recommendation is OUT for core; use stdlib tomllib + a hand-rolled CENTRAL typed Config (dataclass) + validation. NOTE: tomllib is 3.11+, but pyproject target-version is py310 — resolve (bump floor to 3.11, or a minimal optional tomli fallback) WITHOUT adding a hard core dependency.

## Success Criteria
- One typed Config source of truth; no scattered ad-hoc config reads.
- TOML config + documented precedence; bespoke flat parser retired (with back-compat alias window).
- Unknown-key + parse-error handling is loud, not silent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decide + document the config format/precedence/discovery model (ADR-style note in docs/)
- [ ] Resolve the tomllib-vs-py310 floor question (bump to 3.11 OR optional fallback, no hard core dep)
- [ ] Introduce a single typed Config (stdlib dataclass) as the source of truth; route current config-file reads (verify.*, ticket.display_mode) + env reads through it
- [ ] TOML support ([tool.rebar] in pyproject.toml + rebar.toml) with upward discovery + XDG user fallback
- [ ] Back-compat: alias the old flat .rebar/config.conf keys during a deprecation window; warn on unknown keys
- [ ] `rebar config` / --show-config transparency command (resolved values + source layer)
- [ ] Standardize/document REBAR_<KEY> env precedence; audit the ~40 env vars (separate env-var review)
- [ ] Docs (README/CLAUDE.md/docs) updated; full suite green
data.title: Configuration refinement: align rebar config with community conventions
